pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 65-69: namespace and applicationId both set to com.example.weatherapp
Task description specifies creating SyncWorker in app/src/main/java/com/example/weatherapp/sync/SyncWorker.kt; export_result.sh line 9 confirms expected path
Task description specifies creating WeatherApplication in app/src/main/java/com/example/weatherapp/WeatherApplication.kt; export_result.sh line 10 confirms expected path
Task description line 3: 'save the current system time (milliseconds) to SharedPreferences using the name "weather_prefs" and key "last_sync_time"'
Task description line 3: 'using the name "weather_prefs" and key "last_sync_time"'
Task description line 3: 'Enqueue it using `enqueueUniquePeriodicWork` with the name "weather_sync" and policy `KEEP`'

Web search verified this is the official AndroidX WorkManager Kotlin extension library from Android Jetpack. Maven Central and official Android developer documentation (https://developer.android.com/jetpack/androidx/releases/work and https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/androidx.work/work-runtime-ktx/2.9.1) confirm version 2.9.1 exists as specified in task description.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires integrating Android WorkManager into a synthetic WeatherApp project at /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/WeatherApp with app package com.example.weatherapp. Implementation requires: (1) adding androidx.work:work-runtime-ktx 2.9.1 or newer dependency, (2) creating SyncWorker extending CoroutineWorker that saves current system time to SharedPreferences 'weather_prefs' with key 'last_sync_time', (3) creating WeatherApplication extending Application that schedules a PeriodicWorkRequest for SyncWorker running every 15 minutes with NetworkType.CONNECTED constraint using enqueueUniquePeriodicWork with name 'weather_sync' and ExistingPeriodicWorkPolicy.KEEP, (4) registering WeatherApplication in AndroidManifest.xml, and (5) ensuring successful build with assembleDebug. All metadata values are verified against task description, setup/export scripts, and official Android WorkManager documentation.
